prompt:
We're upgrading our payment page to PCI-DSS compliant strict CSP. We have an Alpine component that needs to expand/collapse a 'terms and conditions' panel and inverts a boolean. The current code is `
terms…
`. Convert it to be CSP-safe.

assertions.name: registers-with-Alpine-data
assertions.description: Uses Alpine.data('name', () => ({...})) to register the component (CSP requirement, replaces inline x-data object)

assertions.name: fires-on-alpine-init
assertions.description: Registers the component on the alpine:init event (the canonical CSP-safe registration timing)

assertions.name: x-data-references-name-only
assertions.description: Uses x-data="<name>" referencing a registered component, not an inline object

assertions.name: no-inline-negation
assertions.description: Does NOT use `accepted = !accepted` or any inline negation (forbidden under strict CSP)

assertions.name: uses-method-reference-for-click
assertions.description: @click is a method reference (no parens, no inline expression) like `@click="toggle"`

assertions.name: mentions-createBooleanObject-or-toggle-method
assertions.description: Either uses hyva.createBooleanObject for the boolean or defines a toggle() method on the component

assertions.name: mentions-CSP-context
assertions.description: Mentions CSP, PCI-DSS, or strict CSP — confirms the answer addresses the CSP requirement
